class.name: TextureLayered
class.brief_description: Base class for texture types which represent an array of [Image]s of the same size and format.
class.description: Base class for [Texture2DArray], [Cubemap] and [CubemapArray]. Cannot be used directly, but contains all the functions necessary for accessing the derived resource types. Data is set on a per-layer basis. For [Texture2DArray]s, the layer specifies the array layer. All images need to have the same width, height and number of mipmap levels. A [TextureLayered] can be loaded with [code]method ResourceFormatLoaderTextureLayered.load[/code]. To create a [TextureLayered] texture, an image file needs to be "sliced". Internally, Godot uses the thirdparty basis_universal library by Binomial LLC for e.g. creating and reading [Texture2DArray], [Cubemap] and [CubemapArray] files. Their file extension is normally [code].basis[/code]. Godot adjust this to its needs: [codeblock] - the Texture2DArray becomes a ".tex2darr" - the Cubemap becomes a ".cube" - the CubemapArray becomes a ".cubearr" [/codeblock] The [code]*.basis[/code] file remembers how to apply the slicing. basis_universal can be accessed via the Godot Editor (during the image file import) and the basisu commandline tool (see file [code]thirdparty/basis_universal/basisu_tool.cpp[/code] regarding [code]-tex_type 2darray[/code]). With this library you combine several image files (e.g. png) to one *.basis file. This file will be read and sliced when you load it (see [code]basisu_backend.compute_slice_crcs[/code]). Godot makes use of the loaded slices using the [code]method RenderingDevice.texture_create_shared_from_slice[/code] method using the [code]enum RenderingDevice.TextureSliceType[/code].

method.name: create_from_images
method.description: Creates an array of referenced [Image]s. The first image decides the width, height, image format and mipmapping setting for this [TextureLayered]. The other images must have the same width, height, image format and mipmapping setting. Each [Image] represents one [code]layer[/code].

method.name: _get_images
method.description: Returns an [Array] of [Image] resources, taken from all [code]layers[/code].

method.name: get_format
method.description: Returns the current format being used by this texture. See [enum Image.Format] for details.

method.name: get_height
method.description: Returns the height of the texture. Height is typically represented by the Y-axis.

method.name: get_layer_data
method.description: Returns an [Image] resource with the data from specified [code]layer[/code].

method.name: get_width
method.description: Returns the width of the texture. Width is typically represented by the X-axis.

method.name: update_layer
method.description: Replaces the existing [Image] data at the given [code]layer[/code] with this new image. The given [Image] must have the same width, height, image format and mipmapping setting (a [code]bool[/code] value) as the rest of the referenced images. If the image format is unsupported, it will be decompressed and converted to a similar and supported [enum Image.Format]. The update is immediate: synced with the draw.
